      167    value: Vec<f64>,

● Now fetch() to lock and reuse the static client:

● Bash(gh run list --limit 3 2>&1 | cat)
  ⎿  queued             fix(market_structure): reuse one long-lived exchange client across polls        Errors  v0.2.7  push    29301298042     3s      2026-07-14T02:31:57Z
     queued             fix(market_structure): reuse one long-lived exchange client across polls        Warnings        v0.2.7  push    29301298025     3s      2026-07-14T02:31:57Z
     queued             fix(market_structure): reuse one long-lived exchange client across polls        Release containers      v0.2.7  push    29301297783     3s      2026-07-14T02:31:57Z

  Thought for 19s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Fixed and shipping as v0.2.7 (CI building now). Summary:

  What I got wrong: moving banned_until to a process-global static — you're right, that assumes one egress identity for the whole process and lets one client's ban poison every other client. Reverted; nothing was published (you stopped it before cargo release ran). v_exchanges stays at the published per-client 0.20.6.

  Actual root cause: market_structure_json called exchange_name.init_client() every poll — a fresh client with an empty banned_until. So the gate recorded the ban, then the client was dropped; next poll re-hit Binance, got a fresh 418, and renewed the ban. That's why I watched the unban time march 07:22 → 07:26 live.

  Fix (site-only, no dep bump): hold one long-lived Binance client in a static behind a lock and reuse it across polls, so a recorded ban short-circuits the next poll network-free and the ban expires on its own. collect_data/get_historical_data now borrow &dyn Exchange instead of Arc-cloning a per-poll client.

  And to answer your original question directly: no, it does not crash when the exchange is unavailable — the pod is 1/1 Running, 0 restarts, and the endpoint returns a graceful 500. Your "no containers" was the 5-minute ARM image-pull window during the v0.2.6 rollout, not a panic.

  Once v0.2.7 rolls out I'll verify on the pod that the unban timestamp stops advancing and Binance stops being hit. Let me check the build:

● Bash(gh run list --limit 1 --workflow=release-container.yml 2>&1 | cat)
  ⎿  in_progress                fix(market_structure): reuse one long-lived exchange client across polls        Release containers      v0.2.7  push    29301297783     31s     2026-07-14T02:31:57Z

  Thought for 13s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Build's running (~13 min + ~5 min ARM pull). Let me watch it in the background and verify on the pod once v0.2.7 lands:

● Bash(gh run watch 29301297783 --exit-status >/dev/null 2>&1; echo "BUILD DONE: exit=$?"; echo "waiting for pod to roll to v0.2.7..."; for i in $(seq 1 40); do img=$(…)
  ⎿  Running in the background (↓ to manage)

  Thought for 10s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Monitoring the build + rollout in the background (ID b8xrva72z) — it'll notify me when v0.2.7 is live on the pod, then I'll run the verification (hammer the endpoint, confirm the unban timestamp holds steady and the short-circuit fires instead of re-hitting Binance). I'll report back with the result.
